name: Gina Hinojosa
bio: Regina Inez Hinojosa is an American lawyer and politician who has served in the Texas House of Representatives since 2017, representing the 49th district in central Austin. Born on December 8, 1973 in McAllen, Texas, she previously worked as a civil rights and labor attorney and served as president of the Austin Independent School District Board of Trustees before entering state politics.

stances.summary: Hinojosa advocates using state revenue to fund public schools and infrastructure, explicitly favoring public investment over corporate tax breaks.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa 2026 Texas Democratic-primary candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: our tax revenue should be used to fix our crumbling infrastructure … put our tax dollars back into our people

stances.position_label: Raise on corporations & wealthy to fund services
stances.summary: She calls for ending corporate loopholes and using the proceeds to lower middle-class taxes while protecting neighborhood-school funding.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa 2026 Texas Democratic-primary candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: lower taxes for middle-class families … wipe the slate clean of wasteful vendor contracts

stances.summary: Hinojosa emphasizes supporting workers and small businesses rather than reducing business costs, and she highlights her union-lawyer background.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa 2026 Texas Democratic-primary candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: encourage economic policies that support small businesses and workers rather than tax breaks … for large corporations

stances.position_label: Public support & antitrust vs. big firms
stances.summary: She supports grants/loans for small businesses and vows to curb corporate monopolies that raise costs for consumers.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa 2026 Texas Democratic-primary candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: encourage economic policies that support small businesses … hold corporate monopolies accountable

stances.summary: Hinojosa has consistently supported expanding government healthcare coverage. She voted to extend Medicaid eligibility for 12 months after birth or miscarriage (HB133) and supported a pilot program for Medicaid coverage of doula services (HB158). Her campaign platform emphasizes fighting for affordable health care and strengthening health care access.

sources.title: Record Votes: Rep. Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: Yea | HB133 | Healthcare: Extends Medicaid elegibility for 12 months after birth or miscarriage | Yea | HB158 | Healthcare: Pilot program to provide Medicaid coverage of doula services

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa's Democratic candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: As a civil rights and union lawyer and a former school board president, I've spent my career standing with hardworking Texans. In the Texas House, I helped secure billions of dollars for public schools, fought to strengthen health care access

stances.position_label: Invest in public schools; oppose vouchers
stances.summary: Hinojosa is a strong advocate for public school funding and opposes school vouchers. She authored an opinion piece explicitly stating she opposes the governor's voucher plan, calling it 'irresponsible and harmful.' She emphasizes that Texas public schools need at least $40 billion to reach national per-student funding averages and has worked with bipartisan allies to block voucher legislation.

sources.title: Opinion: Hinojosa: Texas schools need money, not vouchers
sources.quote: Because the governor's voucher plan is irresponsible and harmful to the vast majority of Texas schoolchildren, I oppose it and will not support any 'deal' that breaks from our long-standing Texas tradition of preserving taxpayer dollars exclusively for public schools.

sources.title: Opinion: Hinojosa: Texas schools need money, not vouchers
sources.quote: Texas public schools need at least $40 billion this biennium to reach the national per-student funding average.

stances.summary: Hinojosa's voting record shows consistent support for expanding Medicaid. She voted for HB133 to extend Medicaid eligibility for 12 months postpartum and HB158 for Medicaid coverage of doula services. These votes indicate support for expanding eligibility and benefits rather than restraining program growth.

sources.title: Record Votes: Rep. Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: Yea | HB133 | Healthcare: Extends Medicaid elegibility for 12 months after birth or miscarriage | Yea | HB158 | Healthcare: Pilot program to provide Medicaid coverage of doula services

stances.summary: Hinojosa has a clear pro-choice voting record. She voted 'Nay' on HB1280, the 'backstop' bill to outlaw abortions if Roe v. Wade was overturned, and 'Nay' on SB8, which banned abortion at approximately 6 weeks without exceptions for rape and incest. She also authored a bill relating to exceptions to laws prohibiting abortion.

sources.title: Record Votes: Rep. Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: Nay | HB1280 | Reproductive Rights: 'Backstop' bill outlawing abortions if Roe vs. Wade is overturned | Nay | SB8 | Reproductive Rights: Outlaws abortion at about 6 weeks; no exceptions for rape and incest

sources.title: Bills Authored / Joint Authored - Rep. Gina Hinojosa 89th Legislature
sources.quote: Caption: Relating to an exception to certain laws prohibiting abortion.

stances.summary: Hinojosa identifies as a civil rights lawyer and has built her career on fighting for working families. Her campaign materials emphasize her background in civil rights law and her work standing with marginalized communities. She describes herself as 'one of the most progressive Democrats in the Texas House.'

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa's Democratic candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: As a civil rights and union lawyer and a former school board president, I've spent my career standing with hardworking Texans.

sources.title: Opinion: Hinojosa: Texas schools need money, not vouchers
sources.quote: Despite serving as one of the most progressive Democrats in the Texas House

stances.summary: Hinojosa identifies as a 'union lawyer' and has spent her career working with labor organizations. Her campaign emphasizes fighting for working families rather than corporate interests, and she has opposed policies that divert public resources to wealthy interests.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa's Democratic candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: As a civil rights and union lawyer and a former school board president, I've spent my career standing with hardworking Texans.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa's Democratic candidate questionnaire for Texas Governor
sources.quote: My approach to governing Texas is built on a lifetime of fighting for working families, public education, affordable health care, and real economic opportunity, not corporate handouts.

stances.summary: Hinojosa voted against HB9-2, which would have appropriated state funds for international border control using state funds and staff. This suggests support for federal handling of immigration rather than state-level enforcement expansion.

sources.title: Record Votes: Rep. Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: Nay | HB9-2 | Immigration: Appropriation for international border control using state funds and staff

stances.position_label: Supports prevention and community investment over policing
stances.summary: In a 2020 voter guide questionnaire, Hinojosa indicated support for redirecting funds from police departments to mental health and community services. She did not answer most other criminal justice questions.

sources.title: Voter Guide Profile for TX State Rep., Dist. 49 candidate - Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: I support redirecting funds from police [to] mental health and community

stances.position_label: Supports stricter gun laws including universal background checks and licensing
stances.summary: Hinojosa voted against multiple bills expanding gun rights, including permitless carry (HB1927) and restrictions on governor's emergency powers over gun sales (HB1500). In a 2017 public statement, she proposed ten gun violence prevention measures including universal background checks, license requirements for long guns, and lethal violence protective orders.

sources.title: Record Votes: Rep. Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: ✔ | Nay | HB1927 | Gun Control: Allows gun carry by anyone over 21 and not specifically prohibited

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa on Gun Control
sources.quote: Legislative Outcome: Passed House 82-62-2 on May/23/21; Rep. Gina Hinojosa voted NO

sources.title: We can prevent gun violence in Texas — now
sources.quote: 7. Pass universal background checks. Quick, simple, and if you're a law-abiding citizen, no problem. 9. Require a 'License to Carry' for long guns. 10. Create lethal violence protective orders

stances.position_label: Expand voting access, oppose restrictive measures
stances.summary: Hinojosa consistently votes against Republican-backed voting restriction bills and supports expanding access. She voted against SB7 (2021) which limited voting methods, against HB3920 tightening disability mail voting restrictions, and participated in quorum breaks to protest redistricting. She frames voting access as a fundamental democratic right and criticizes Republican efforts as 'rigging' elections.

sources.title: Record Votes: Rep. Gina Hinojosa
sources.quote: ✔ | Nay | HB3920 | Democracy: Tightens restrictions on mail voting due to disability | ✔ | Nay | SB7 | Democracy: Limits voting in several ways; forbids many methods of making voting easier

sources.title: A Texas Democratic lawmaker on their efforts to stop Republican redistricting plans
sources.quote: It is undemocratic. It is anti the foundations of our country, and it is extreme and harmful to our constituents. So we will do everything in our power to fight it.

stances.position_label: Supports evolving Constitution, criticizes originalist approach
stances.summary: Hinojosa criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Louisiana v. Callais (2026) which narrowed Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, framing it as allowing politicians to 'rig our elections.' She supports judges who protect voting rights and representation, opposing decisions that restrict constitutional protections.

sources.title: STATEMENT: Hinojosa on Supreme Court's Decision in Louisiana v. Callais
sources.quote: Texans and Americans are fighting back, but today the Supreme Court is trying to give politicians the power to rig our elections for a generation.

qualitative.summary: No corruption investigations, indictments, ethics violations, or impeachment proceedings involving Regina Inez Hinojosa were found in public records. The search results instead concern former Congressman Rubén Hinojosa, who filed for personal bankruptcy in 2010 over a family business loan guarantee, and Texas Democratic Party Chairman Gilberto Hinojosa, who is a different individual.

sources.title: Rep. Gina Hinojosa joins Democratic race for Texas governor
House Dem files for bankruptcy

qualitative.summary: Hinojosa has measurable favorability data from December 2025 polling by the University of Texas/Texas Politics Project, though specific favorable/unfavorable percentages were not fully extracted from the source. Her constituent service reputation is suggested by her focus on public education and healthcare funding, including securing nearly $1 billion in federal funding for indigent healthcare in Travis County.

sources.title: Gina Hinojosa Favorability (December 2025)
Rep. Gina Hinojosa joins Democratic race for Texas governor

qualitative.summary: Hinojosa has filed annual financial disclosure forms as required for state legislators, though specific details from these filings were not retrieved in the search. No FOIA or press access violations were found. Her campaign launch included public documentation of her legislative record and policy positions.

sources.title: Rep. Gina Hinojosa joins Democratic race for Texas governor

qualitative.summary: Hinojosa has held elected office since 2012, serving first on the Austin ISD school board (including as board president) before winning election to the Texas House in 2016. She is now in her fifth term as a state representative. Her legislative record includes passing bills to reduce standardized testing, increase teacher pay, and secure federal healthcare funding. She has served as a visible opponent to Governor Abbott's school voucher program.

sources.title: Rep. Gina Hinojosa joins Democratic race for Texas governor
